page.title: Yunnan Himalaya Foothills — Meili, Kawagebo & the Snub-nosed Monkey

seo.metaDescription: A focused 7-day Lijiang-based deep dive into the Northwest Yunnan of legends. Black Dragon Pond, Hidden Jade Dragon, Tiger Leaping Gorge, Songzanlin, the Great Bend of the Yangtze, Kawagebo sunrise, and the rare Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey.

sections.subtitle: Meili Snow Mountain, off the beaten path
sections.text: A focused 7-day Lijiang-based dive into the heart of Northwest Yunnan. The classic Old Town and Black Dragon Pond opening. The hidden Jade Dragon trail behind the rope-line. The legendary Tiger Leaping Gorge. North to Meili Snow Mountain for the first light on Kawagebo (6,740m). The Great Bend of the Yangtze. A real chance at the rare Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey in its only habitat. Plus Cizhong's Catholic church, the Dongzhulin Monastery, a Himalayan bee farm, and a Lisu bonfire party.

items.title: Headline Target
items.subtitle: Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey
items.highlight: Critically endangered primate, found only in NW Yunnan - observed in their natural Baima Snow Mountain habitat

items.title: Duration
items.subtitle: Seven Days
items.highlight: Day 1: 10 AM to 9 PM | Days 2-6: 8 AM to 9 PM | Day 7: 9 AM to 2 PM

items.title: Locations
items.subtitle: Lijiang - Wenhai - Hutiao - Shangri-La - Deqin - Cizhong - Tacheng - Lijiang

items.title: Activity Level
items.subtitle: Moderate
items.highlight: Daily walking 5-10 km, plus Meili viewing treks at altitude

items.title: Snub-nosed Monkeys in the Wild
items.subtitle: Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve
items.text: Visit the Weixi-Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey National Park - the world's only natural habitat for this critically endangered primate (fewer than 3,000 individuals). Observe them in their natural environment with a local conservation ranger, then learn about the conservation work at the Baima Snow Mountain Nature Conservation Bureau.

items.title: Kawagebo Sunrise
items.subtitle: Meili Snow Mountain
items.text: A pre-dawn visit to the Feilai Monastery viewpoint for the spectacular sunrise on Kawagebo - Yunnan's highest peak at 6,740m. On a clear morning, the alpine glow lasts 15-20 minutes and stays with you for years.

items.title: Great Bend of the Yangtze
items.subtitle: UNESCO World Heritage
items.text: Witness the dramatic First Bend of the Yangtze where the river makes a sharp U-turn at Shigu. The Three Parallel Rivers of Yunnan Protected Area is a UNESCO World Heritage site - one of the most biodiverse temperate regions on earth.

items.title: Cizhong Catholic Church
items.subtitle: Cizhong Village
items.text: Visit Cizhong Village - a remote village on the Mekong River where French Catholic missionaries built a 100-year-old church, still active today, surrounded by the local Lisu minority's vineyards. A working cultural fusion, not a museum.

items.title: Lisu Bonfire + Tibetan Reba Dance
items.subtitle: Tacheng
items.text: A traditional Lisu minority bonfire night in Tacheng, with the energetic Reba dance performance - full of colors, ancient rhythms, and the kind of cultural connection that only happens in the smaller villages.

items.title: Himalayan Bee Farm
items.tagline: Indigenous giant honeybee of the Himalaya
items.subtitle: Tacheng
items.text: Visit a local apiarist working with the Himalayan giant honeybee (Apis dorsata laboriosa) - a species that nests at extreme altitudes. Learn about the harvest, sample the prized honey, and understand the community's sustainable relationship with these remarkable insects.

items.title: Lijiang Black Dragon Pond
items.subtitle: Jade Dragon backdrop
items.text: Day 1 afternoon at Black Dragon Pond Park - the iconic Pavilion of Embracing the Moon with Jade Dragon Snow Mountain as the backdrop, reflecting in still water. A scene that has drawn Chinese painters for centuries.

image.altText: Black Dragon Pond with Jade Dragon backdrop

items.title: Dongba Museum & Naxi Culture
items.subtitle: Black Dragon Pond next door
items.text: The Dongba Museum next to Black Dragon Pond - introduces the Dongba pictograph script, the only pictographic writing system still in use in the world. Cared for by the local Naxi community; an active cultural site, not a museum.

items.title: Wenhai & Hidden Jade Dragon
items.subtitle: Highland plateau
items.text: Day 2 trek to Wenhai and the hidden trails of the Jade Dragon Snow Mountain Nature Reserve, behind the rope-line, away from the cable car crowds. Naxi-minority hamlets, alpine meadows, and views of the south face of the snow range.

items.title: Tiger Leaping Gorge
items.subtitle: Earth's deepest gorge
items.text: A 4-km hike through the legendary Tiger Leaping Gorge - one of the deepest river canyons on Earth, where the Jinsha River roars between snow-capped mountains. A Yunnan must-do.

items.title: Songzanlin Monastery
items.subtitle: Tibetan Buddhism centre
items.text: The magnificent Songzanlin Monastery - the most important Tibetan Buddhist monastery in Yunnan, often called the 'Little Potala Palace' for its grand scale. Active monastery, monks in residence, prayer halls open to visitors.

items.title: Jinsha River Bend at Benzilan
items.tagline: First Great Bend of the Yangtze
items.subtitle: UNESCO Three Parallel Rivers
items.text: Stop at the dramatic Jinsha River Bend at Benzilan - the First Great Bend of the Yangtze, where the river makes a sharp U-turn. Part of the UNESCO Three Parallel Rivers of Yunnan Protected Area - one of the most biodiverse temperate regions on Earth.

image.altText: First Bend of the Yangtze at Benzilan

activities: Pickup at Lijiang Sanyi Airport (LJG) or your Old Town hotel, transfer to your boutique Naxi courtyard hotel. Afternoon at Black Dragon Pond Park with the iconic Moon-Embracing Pavilion and stunning views of Jade Dragon Snow Mountain, plus the Dongba Museum next door - Naxi pictographs and culture. Stroll through the lively Zhongyi Market and the cobblestone streets of Dayan Old Town. Welcome dinner.

Drive to the Wenhai area on the Jade Dragon Snow Mountain plateau. Trek into the hidden trails of the Jade Dragon Snow Mountain Nature Reserve - off the rope-line, away from the cable car crowds. Villages and Naxi minority hamlets with a resident local guide, birdwatching and nature photography along the way. Return to Lijiang, dinner in Old Town.

days.title: Lashi Wetlands + Tiger Leaping Gorge + Shangri-La

activities: Morning at Lashi Wetlands - dawn crane flight, waterfowl, Black Drongo roosts. Continue north toward Shangri-La. 4-km hike through the legendary Tiger Leaping Gorge (Hutiaoxia) - one of the deepest river canyons on Earth. Arrive in mystical Shangri-La. Dukezong Old Town - the spiritual heart of Tibetan Yunnan. Dinner and overnight.

Visit the magnificent Songzanlin Monastery - the 'Little Potala Palace', a major centre of Tibetan Buddhism. Drive north via Benzilan to the dramatic Jinsha River Bend (the First Great Bend of the Yangtze). Visit the peaceful Dongzhulin Monastery. Arrive in Deqin - the gateway town to Meili Snow Mountain. Dinner and overnight in the snow-capped mountain setting.

days.title: Kawagebo Sunrise + Cizhong Village + Mekong Walk

activities: Pre-dawn departure to the Feilai Monastery viewpoint for sunrise on Kawagebo (6,740m) - Yunnan's highest peak. A clear-morning alpine glow that stays with you for years. Visit Cizhong Village on the Mekong River - the 100-year-old French Catholic church, still active, surrounded by Lisu-minority vineyards. Walk along the Mekong. Traditional dinner paired with local Cizhong wine. Overnight in Cizhong or drive back to Deqin.

days.title: Snub-nosed Monkeys + Great Bend Return + Lijiang

activities: Early departure to the Weixi-Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey National Park. Trek into the habitat with a local conservation ranger to observe the critically endangered Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkeys (fewer than 3,000 in the world). Lunch at a local rainbow trout farm, then visit the Baima Snow Mountain Nature Conservation Bureau for insights into the conservation work. Drive south along the Yangtze, witness the dramatic First Bend of the Yangtze, arrive in Lijiang. Farewell dinner at a Naxi courtyard restaurant in Lijiang Old Town.

Optional: explore more of Lijiang Old Town or Black Dragon Pond Park, OR drive to Tacheng (additional half-day add-on). Afternoon in Tacheng: visit a local Himalayan bee farm - learn about the indigenous giant honeybee (Apis dorsata laboriosa) and sample prized high-altitude honey. Traditional Tibetan bonfire and Reba dance performance - colorful, ancient, and wonderfully un-touristy. Transfer back to Lijiang for final overnight.

contactNote.text: For groups of 1 person or more than 6 people

included.text: Lead Coordinator (Lynne Lyu or Lukasz Madrzynski), Local Guides at each stop (Naxi, Tibetan, Lisu), All Accommodation (3-4 star hotels and traditional guesthouses), All Meals (B/L/D), Private Transportation, All Park + Reserve Entrance Fees, Conservation Bureau + Snub-nosed Monkey Park + Feilai Viewpoint access, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: International Flights to/from Lijiang, Personal Expenses, Voluntary Gratuities, Optional Tacheng half-day add-on
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 10 AM at Lijiang Sanyi Airport (LJG) | Return: 9 PM at Lijiang Sanyi Airport

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: What is the success rate for seeing Snub-nosed Monkeys?
items.answer: Near-100% on Day 6 morning at the Weixi-Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey National Park with a local ranger guide. The monkeys are habituated to researchers and we observe them in the wild (not at a zoo).

items.question: Is the Kawagebo sunrise guaranteed?
items.answer: Weather-dependent - clear mornings are required. We schedule it for Day 5 but build in flexibility. If Day 5 is cloudy, our guide will try again on the return trip. We will not skip it just for a tighter schedule - this is the headline of the trip.

items.question: What about altitude in Deqin and Meili?
items.answer: Deqin sits at 3,550m, the Feilai viewpoint is at 3,800m. The itinerary climbs gradually (Lijiang 2,400m -> Shangri-La 3,300m -> Deqin 3,550m) to acclimatize. Lukasz and Lynne are trained in high-altitude travel and we carry oxygen and emergency medication.

items.question: How do we visit the Snub-nosed Monkey park?
items.answer: The Weixi-Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey National Park is a working research site. We partner with the local conservation rangers who take small groups on a controlled walk into the habitat. The monkeys are wild (not caged) but habituated to researchers.

items.question: What is the typical group size?
items.answer: 2-6 guests. Smaller groups work better for this trip because some of the access points (Cizhong, the monkey park, Meili viewing) have limited capacity.

items.question: Can this trip be combined with the Yunnan Four Kingdoms loop?
items.answer: Yes - the two itineraries connect naturally at Lijiang. A combined 12-14 day tour (Kunming to Lijiang via the Grand Loop, then the Meili deep dive) is a popular option. Surcharge for the combined trip is ~$1,000-1,200 per person.

items.question: What is the best season for this tour?
items.answer: October-December is prime (clear skies, autumn colors, best chance of clear Kawagebo sunrise). March-May also works (spring blossoms, more rain risk for sunrise). June-September is monsoon - lower success rate for the mountain views.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation and local communities?
items.answer: Park fees contribute to the Weixi-Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey National Park. We use locally-owned hotels, restaurants, and Lisu/Tibetan homestays. The Cizhong church visit and Tacheng bee farm directly support those communities. We contribute trip data to local conservation bureaus for species monitoring.
